name: Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive in real time

parameters.text: =✅ Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

notion — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Fetch Changes (Google Drive) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Fetch Changes (Google Drive)

googleDrive — `fileFolder` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Normalize Records · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Normalize Records

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming payload records to a stable schema

Next: Upsert into Google Sheets

### Upsert into Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Advance Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Advance Watermark

Custom JavaScript: Advance the incremental watermark to the newest record seen

Next: Persist Watermark (Notion)

### Persist Watermark (Notion)

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Guard Non-Empty source · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Guard Non-Empty source

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Sort Payloads Newest First

### Sort Payloads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive in real time

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual helpers is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Load Previous Snapshot → Fetch Changes (Google Drive) → Normalize Records → Upsert into Google Sheets → Advance Watermark → Persist Watermark (Notion) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating helpers in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your helpers process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
